name: nest
description: LLM-optimized folder structure design. Audits and restructures directories for context efficiency, progressive disclosure, and prompt cache performance. Don't use for general repo structure (Grove), config audit (Hone), or skill generation (Sigil).

capabilities.summary: Evaluate existing folder layout against LLM navigation efficiency criteria
Design L1/L2/L3 directory hierarchies for layered context loading
Place CLAUDE.md and rules files at optimal project levels
Arrange static-first file ordering for prompt cache hit maximization
Apply file/folder naming conventions that improve LLM grep and glob success
Distribute content across files to stay within per-file token targets

recipes.label: Structure Audit
recipes.when: LLM navigation efficiency audit of existing folder structure

recipes.label: Restructure
recipes.when: Restructuring for LLM optimization (includes git mv execution)

recipes.label: Prompt Cache
recipes.when: Prompt cache topology optimization and static-file-first ordering

recipes.label: Naming
recipes.when: File and folder naming audit for LLM grep/glob discoverability — bias-correction for generic names (utils, helpers), domain-vs-type grouping, suffix conventions (.config, .test, .spec), case strategy (kebab/camel/Pascal), rename-impact analysis

recipes.label: Sharding
recipes.when: Large file sharding strategy — split CLAUDE.md / reference docs via @import, choose split axis (by domain / by phase / by frequency), preserve cache prefixes, design include manifest, validate cycle-free imports

recipes.label: Monorepo
recipes.when: Monorepo workspace topology for LLM efficiency — package boundaries (apps/, packages/, libs/), per-workspace CLAUDE.md cascade, turborepo / nx / pnpm-workspace path optimization, shared rule deduplication
